author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Shipped on branch `v3-read-path` in `~/p/jjforge-v3-read`. One commit:
`storage: v3 git-only read path`.

Repointed at the v3 ref layout under `StorageMode::V3`:
`Storage::read`, `list_ids`, `list_ready`, `list_memories`,
`read_memory`, `resolve`, and `read_history`. V2 reads keep their
bookmark routing intact.

Added `git.walk_commits` and `history::read_history_at_v3` — the v3
op-replay walks `refs/jjf/issues/<id>` via `git log`, feeds the
unchanged Jjf-Op trailer parser, sorts via the v2 LWW key. The
debug-build file-read vs op-replay cross-check ports over and runs
against the per-issue ref's chain on V3 repos.

V3 reads bypass the snapshot cache (which is bookmark-shaped); ticket
`aa915fe` (v3-cache) will port the cache to a ref-set-derived key.
Bypass cost is N git calls per `list_ready`; sub-second on ~1k-issue
corpora, fine for the live planner today.

Acceptance: 13 new V3 read-path integration tests
(`crates/jjf-storage/tests/v3_read_path.rs`) cover round-trip,
mutation reflection, list/filter, slug resolve, memory CRUD, history
walking, and the cross-check panic on injected divergence. Workspace
`cargo nextest run --workspace` is 444 / 444 green.

## Recommendation

Close the ticket. All five acceptance boxes tick.

## Confidence

High. The trailer parser stays unchanged; v3 just supplies a
different commit source. The debug cross-check fires loudly on
divergence — verified with a `#[should_panic]` test that injects an
unflagged snapshot.

## Open follow-ups

- Cache port (ticket `aa915fe` / v3-cache): `read`, `list_ready`,
  `resolve`, `list_memories` currently bypass the cache on V3.
  Port should derive the cache key from `for-each-ref` shas and
  reuse the existing rebuild shape.
- `read_history_at(rev, id)` (used by the merge driver) is still V2-
  only: V3's "rev" concept doesn't translate to a bookmark revset.
  Merge driver work in a later ticket needs to re-think this signature.
- Init rewrite (ticket `add0646`) still needs to plant the sentinel
  ref on fresh repos; today tests plant it by hand.
- Performance opt to batch `cat-file --batch` for `list_ready` —
  defer until cache port lands and N gets large enough to matter.
